content_head:
你是我的个人信息流分析助理。请基于下方 Cloud Vault browser-capture 材料，
        生成 2026-07-22 的中文 Markdown 日报。

        约束:
        - 只使用材料里出现的信息，不要编造外部事实。
        - 明确区分“事实信息”“原作者观点”“需要验证的问题”。
        - 投资相关内容只做研究观察，不给买卖建议、仓位建议或交易指令。
        - 优先提取对 AI 基建、半导体/PCB/光通信、工具链、市场情绪、个人效率有用的信号。
        - 对低信噪比、重复、鸡汤、缺少来源的内容要直接降权。
        - 输出要适合 Telegram 阅读：重点清晰，避免长篇复述。

        建议结构:
        # Cloud Vault 每日信息流 - 2026-07-22

        ### 1. @francoisfleuret I don't think it would be very hard, but there are nuances. Here is some back-of-the-napkin thoughts: Assuming we're talking about standard hardware (not Nvidia...
                - Capture ID: ai-x:2079615333522878672
                - 时间: 2026-07-22 01:30
                - 来源: x.com / post
                - 作者: EdSealing
                - 标签: ai-x-discovery, cxl_datacenter, risk_warning, Memory
                - 优先级: urgent

                - 本地路径: inbox/browser-capture/2026-07-22/aix_2079615333522878672.md

                @francoisfleuret I don't think it would be very hard, but there are nuances. Here is some back-of-the-napkin thoughts:
Assuming we're talking about standard hardware (not Nvidia CPU<>GPU NVLink bus, which requires Grace processor), you'd need a processor that can handle that many PCIe lanes. The best current AMD CPU has 128 lanes of PCIe 5.
- Each NVMe needs 4-lanes. So 16x4 = 64 lanes.
- Single GPU needs 16-lanes
So 80 lanes total for the M.2 & GPU. That shoud leave plenty for the rest of the system... doable with 1 GPU
That's just a single GPU though. If you wanted to have multiple GPUs in a system, each adds 16x lanes.
So on an 8xGPU system, that's 128-lanes just for the GPUs. In order to accommodate that, you now need to have multiple processors. This introduces a new bottleneck of inter-processor communication.
With 2 CPUs, you can theoretically have 16xM.2 and 8xGPUs, but you would need to have GPUDirect-Storage and the data would need to be properly sharded across the drives and read into the GPU on the appropriate NUMA. This is more of a software & driver issue than a hardware limitation.
Since the GPUs only have ~141GB-288GB HMB3e each, it's not a massive amount of data to populate and a typical server would have 2TB-4TB of memory.
So, in theory it's possible, but you don't see it very often because it's just easier to give the node more memory (especially with new CXL standards) and just move the data into memory.
Again, just thoughts off the cuff here. There are more advanced technology and things that can be used.

---

                One that doesn't get much coverage, $FN

Market cap: ~$19.1B
Next-quarter guide: ~$1.27B (+4.6% QoQ, +39.6% YoY)
My next 4q revenue estimate: $5.5–6.0B, 30–40% growth
My exit margins: 12.5–13% adjusted gross, 11–12% adjusted operating

Fabrinet sits in the manufacturing layer.

Rather than betting on one laser, DSP, module supplier or architecture, FN manufactures across the ecosystem.

Their exposure includes:
> Optical components and subassemblies
> 800G and 1.6T modules
> DCI and coherent optics
> CPO and external light sources (ELS)
> Optical circuit switches (OCS)
> Advanced packaging

The current momentum is already strong as they grew revenue 39% last quarter to $1.21B, while DCI revenue grew 90%. Datacom demand also exceeded shipments because lasers, memory and advanced chips remained supply constrained. 

That means supply improvement alone can unlock additional revenue.

Two direct hyperscaler 800G programs have completed qualification and begun shipping, while merchant transceiver programs should add another layer of growth through fiscal 2027.

Outside optics, HPC board revenue is also ramping and should eventually exceed $150M per quarter.

As customers outsource more packaging, assembly, testing and full-system
